author: @bhamjoe
text: Hello, I love how easy you make this process. But could you update your links to the ink and solution? They are no longer functional. Thank you.

author: @DaaanielJaaaackson
text:
Hi, I'm trying this for the second time but I'm having a little trouble so I'd love to know specifically where to find
A: the conductive ink that you use (the stuff I got is too watery) and yours came out perfect
B: the universal plating solution (what is it's chemical formula)
When I type universal plating solution into Google there's a massive verity of things that come up under the search but none of them say universal plating solution.
I hope you respond and your video is fantastic, thanks for sharing and I really hope you respond.. regards Dan

author: @i.b.deplorable
text: EXCELLENT VIDEO !!!   Thank you so much.  I never knew that That was possible. I can't wait to try it out.

author: @tarquinbristow
text: After scouring the internet I discover that you of all people have made just what I was looking for! Thank you Robert.

author: @davidcole8122
text:
Hello,
I am wanting to order a couple of items via your website but had a couple of questions and completed the online enquiry form. I’m a bit concerned that the message hasn’t got through as I haven’t heard back (message sent at the beginning of the week). My questions were concerning the improved ink. I wanted to know if it could be mixed with the older ink as I still have some left. The other question was regarding the plating solution. I wanted to know if the brightener part needs refreshing over time/use?
I am getting down to some serious plating and electroforming now and hope to offer some courses and instruction. As I have been very impressed with your ink, I would naturally like to recommend it/sell it together with the plating solution.

author: @michellewhite6992
text: Is it essential to put the graphite ink on the leaf? Or does this ink come in different colours. Im asking this as ive seen a lady on Instagram whose leaves still look green or autumn colours she then enamel paints them. I wish i could send pics on here so i could show you.

author: @davepearson8476
text: thank you for sharing, that was very helpful and informative!

author: @davidcole8122
text: I’m very impressed with your ink. I have been making electroplated jewellery and experimenting with various conductive paints, but this must be the best I have come across for leaves etc. Although I am not an expert on plating, I am about to start making some vids on how to do it reasonably well and avoid the pitfalls that I have surmounted by trial and error. I am using a standard acid-type electrolyte at the moment and will be giving yours a go in the near future.

author: @sharonwood8085
text:
Hello, 
I am very new to electroplating and wondered if someone could possibly answer this question as I’m a little scared of blowing up the Garrage or poisoning the family 🤔
Would you be able to use a swimming pool liquid algicide as a plating solution if the active constituent 60g/L Copper (Cu) present as copper sulphate Pentahydrate? 
Any help would be really great thank you 😊

author: @a0cdhd
text: I wonder if your ink is fine enough to plate a leaf skeleton without clogging up the interstitial spaces between the lignin branches.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: it should be mate - but sometimes the only way to be for sure is to give it a go - If you - can you do a video?

replies.author: @a0cdhd
replies.text: Desmond Bagley Hmmm. OK. AgNO3 + NaOH + NH3 + C12H22O11 + leaf + heat. Dispose of waste thoughtfully, environmentally... All that safety stuff,  and before it goes bang. Never made the association. Thanks, I'll try it.
